error_signature: sticky position not applying to tabs headers
title: Sticky position not applying to tabs headers
symptom: The headers of the tabs element do not stick to the top when scrolling, despite setting the position to sticky.
applies_when: Trying to set headers to sticky position
root_cause: The default styling of the tabs element may not support sticky positioning without additional custom CSS. The container for the headers might not be correctly targeted, leading to the expected behavior not being applied.
fix_steps: Open the HTML Embed section in the editor.
Add the following custom CSS: .tabs-object .tabs-container { position: sticky !important; top: 0; }
Save the changes and preview the tabs element.
agent_instruction: Add custom CSS to the HTML Embed to make the tabs headers sticky.
